qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) via Workflow Import from URL Endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via the workflow import endpoint (/rest/workflows/from-url), while the advisory describes RCE via the Merge node's SQL query mode. These are different vulnerable components and different vulnerability classes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes Remote Code Execution via the Merge node's SQL query mode in n8n. The only qualifying finding identifies SSRF via a different endpoint (/rest/workflows/from-url), which is a different vulnerability class and different vulnerable component.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wxx7-mcgf-j869 states: 'An authenticated user with permission to create or modify workflows could leverage the Merge node's SQL query mode to execute arbitrary code'
Advisory affected component: Merge node in n8n workflow automation platform
Advisory CWE: CWE-89 (SQL Injection) and CWE-94 (Code Injection)
ssrf-workflow-import finding: SSRF via workflow import endpoint, not Merge node SQL query mode
No finding references the Merge node, SQL query mode, or the specific vulnerable code path described in the advisory
